1	"""I-bug-107 — aggregate N BEAT-BOTH runs into mean ± stddev per dimension.
2	
3	A single BEAT-BOTH run has high variance (sweep-to-sweep recovery rates
4	differ ±5pp on identical input due to LLM nondeterminism). For
5	benchmark publication and Carney-grade quality reporting, average N
6	runs and report mean ± stddev per dimension.
7	
8	This script does NOT run sweeps — sweep orchestration is user-budget
9	gated (each full sweep is ~$0.10 OpenRouter spend). It aggregates
10	N pre-existing `outputs/m_live_2_beat_both/manifest.json` artifacts
11	that were produced by separate `scripts/run_m_live_2_beat_both.py`
12	invocations.
13	
14	Usage:
15	    python scripts/aggregate_beat_both_runs.py \\
16	        --manifest path/to/run1/manifest.json \\
17	        --manifest path/to/run2/manifest.json \\
18	        --manifest path/to/run3/manifest.json \\
19	        --output outputs/m_live_2_aggregate/manifest.json
20	
21	Output schema (mirrors single-run schema with aggregate fields):
22	    {
23	        "n_runs": 3,
24	        "input_manifests": [path1, path2, path3],
25	        "polaris_scores_aggregate": {
26	            "<dimension>": {
27	                "mean": float,
28	                "stddev": float,
29	                "min": float,
30	                "max": float,
31	                "values": [v1, v2, v3]
32	            }
33	        },
34	        "chatgpt_scores": {...},     # from first manifest (baseline; identical across runs)
35	        "gemini_scores": {...},      # from first manifest (baseline; identical across runs)
36	        "per_dimension_verdicts": {
37	            "<dimension>": {
38	                "polaris_mean": float,
39	                "polaris_stddev": float,
40	                "chatgpt": float | None,    # None when competitor missing dim
41	                "gemini": float | None,     # None when competitor missing dim
42	                "verdict": "BEAT-BOTH" | "BEAT-ONE" | "TIE" | "BEHIND" | "BEHIND-BOTH" | "N/A" | "INCOMPLETE",
43	                "robust": bool   # True iff (mean - stddev) > both competitors strictly
44	            }
45	        }
46	    }
47	"""
48	
49	from __future__ import annotations
50	
51	import argparse
52	import json
53	import statistics
54	import sys
55	from pathlib import Path
56	from typing import Any
57	
58	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
59	if str(REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
60	    sys.path.insert(0, str(REPO_ROOT))
61	
62	# I-bug-107 iter-1 diff P1 fix: reuse canonical per-dimension tolerance
63	# from scripts/run_m_live_2_beat_both.py / beat_both_scoring.py instead
64	# of a hand-rolled 1% relative tolerance. Canonical tolerance reflects
65	# domain knowledge per dimension (narrative_length tolerance is much
66	# wider than e.g. unique_citations).
67	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.beat_both_scoring import (  # noqa: E402
68	    tolerance_for,
69	)
70	
71	
72	def _load_manifest(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
73	    """Load a single BEAT-BOTH manifest. Validates the expected schema."""
74	    if not path.exists():
75	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"manifest not found: {path}")
76	    data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
77	    required = {"polaris_scores", "chatgpt_scores", "gemini_scores"}
78	    missing = required - set(data.keys())
79	    if missing:
80	        raise ValueError(
81	            f"manifest {path} missing required keys: {sorted(missing)}"
82	        )
83	    return data
84	
85	
86	def _extract_polaris_dimension_values(
87	    manifests: list[dict[str, Any]],
88	) -> dict[str, list[float]]:
89	    """Group polaris_scores values by dimension across N manifests.
90	
91	    Returns: {dimension_name: [run1_value, run2_value, ...]}
92	    """
93	    by_dim: dict[str, list[float]] = {}
94	    for manifest in manifests:
95	        for dim_name, dim_score in manifest["polaris_scores"].items():
96	            value = dim_score.get("value")
97	            if value is None:
98	                continue
99	            by_dim.setdefault(dim_name, []).append(float(value))
100	    return by_dim
101	
102	
103	def _aggregate_polaris_scores(
104	    manifests: list[dict[str, Any]],
105	) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
106	    """Compute per-dimension mean / stddev / min / max across N runs."""
107	    by_dim = _extract_polaris_dimension_values(manifests)
108	    aggregate: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
109	    for dim_name, values in by_dim.items():
110	        if not values:
111	            continue
112	        mean = statistics.mean(values)
113	        stddev = statistics.stdev(values) if len(values) >= 2 else 0.0
114	        aggregate[dim_name] = {
115	            "mean": round(mean, 4),
116	            "stddev": round(stddev, 4),
117	            "min": min(values),
118	            "max": max(values),
119	            "values": values,
120	            "n": len(values),
121	        }
122	    return aggregate
123	
124	
125	def _compute_robust_verdicts(
126	    polaris_aggregate: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
127	    chatgpt_scores: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
128	    gemini_scores: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
129	) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
130	    """Per-dimension verdict using mean ± stddev for robustness.
131	
132	    A verdict is "robust BEAT-BOTH" iff (mean - stddev) > both
133	    competitor scores (i.e., even the worst-case run beats both).
134	
135	    Otherwise the verdict uses the mean directly with the original
136	    BEAT-BOTH / BEAT-ONE / TIE / BEHIND taxonomy.
137	    """
138	    verdicts: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
139	    for dim_name, agg in polaris_aggregate.items():
140	        polaris_mean = agg["mean"]
141	        polaris_stddev = agg["stddev"]
142	        # I-bug-107 iter-1 P2 fix: explicitly handle missing competitor
143	        # dimensions instead of fabricating 0.0. If either competitor
144	        # didn't score this dimension, the verdict can't be computed.
145	        chatgpt_score = chatgpt_scores.get(dim_name)
146	        gemini_score = gemini_scores.get(dim_name)
147	        if chatgpt_score is None or gemini_score is None:
148	            verdicts[dim_name] = {
149	                "polaris_mean": polaris_mean,
150	                "polaris_stddev": polaris_stddev,
151	                "polaris_worst_case": round(polaris_mean - polaris_stddev, 4),
152	                "chatgpt": None,
153	                "gemini": None,
154	                "verdict": "INCOMPLETE",
155	                "robust": False,
156	                "reason": (
157	                    f"competitor missing dimension: "
158	                    f"chatgpt={chatgpt_score is None}, "
159	                    f"gemini={gemini_score is None}"
160	                ),
161	            }
162	            continue
163	        chatgpt_val = chatgpt_score.get("value", 0.0)
164	        gemini_val = gemini_score.get("value", 0.0)
165	
166	        # I-bug-107 iter-1 diff P2 fix: canonical all-zero N/A guard
167	        # (mirrors scripts/run_m_live_2_beat_both.py:492-494). When all
168	        # three scored 0.0, the dimension is structurally unmeasurable
169	        # — emit N/A, not TIE.
170	        if polaris_mean == 0.0 and chatgpt_val == 0.0 and gemini_val == 0.0:
171	            verdicts[dim_name] = {
172	                "polaris_mean": polaris_mean,
173	                "polaris_stddev": polaris_stddev,
174	                "polaris_worst_case": round(polaris_mean - polaris_stddev, 4),
175	                "chatgpt": chatgpt_val,
176	                "gemini": gemini_val,
177	                "verdict": "N/A",
178	                "robust": False,
179	                "rationale": (
180	                    "All 3 manifests scored 0.0 — dimension not "
181	                    "measurable on current inputs."
182	                ),
183	            }
184	            continue
185	
186	        # I-bug-107 iter-1 diff P1 fix: use canonical per-dimension
187	        # tolerance (`tolerance_for(dim)`) — same as the single-run
188	        # verdict path. NOT a hand-rolled 1% rule.
189	        # I-bug-107 iter-2 diff P1 fix: use canonical ahead/behind/tie
190	        # taxonomy (mirrors scripts/run_m_live_2_beat_both.py:527-543).
191	        # Ahead-one + behind-one MUST resolve to BEHIND, NOT BEAT-ONE.
192	        tol = tolerance_for(dim_name)
193	
194	        def _cmp_one(competitor_val: float) -> str:
195	            delta = polaris_mean - competitor_val
196	            if abs(delta) <= tol:
197	                return "tie"
198	            return "ahead" if delta > 0 else "behind"
199	
200	        vs_chatgpt = _cmp_one(chatgpt_val)
201	        vs_gemini = _cmp_one(gemini_val)
202	        ahead_count = sum(1 for v in (vs_chatgpt, vs_gemini) if v == "ahead")
203	        behind_count = sum(1 for v in (vs_chatgpt, vs_gemini) if v == "behind")
204	
205	        if ahead_count == 2:
206	            verdict = "BEAT-BOTH"
207	        elif ahead_count == 1 and behind_count == 0:
208	            verdict = "BEAT-ONE"
209	        elif behind_count == 2:
210	            verdict = "BEHIND-BOTH"
211	        elif behind_count >= 1:
212	            verdict = "BEHIND"
213	        else:
214	            verdict = "TIE"
215	
216	        # I-bug-107 iter-1 P1 fix: robust = STRICT (mean - stddev) > both
217	        # competitor scores. NO tolerance — acceptance specifies strict
218	        # inequality. A near-margin worst_case=100.5 vs competitor=100
219	        # MUST count as robust=True.
220	        worst_case = polaris_mean - polaris_stddev
221	        robust = worst_case > chatgpt_val and worst_case > gemini_val
222	
223	        verdicts[dim_name] = {
224	            "polaris_mean": polaris_mean,
225	            "polaris_stddev": polaris_stddev,
226	            "polaris_worst_case": round(worst_case, 4),
227	            "chatgpt": chatgpt_val,
228	            "gemini": gemini_val,
229	            "verdict": verdict,
230	            "robust": robust,
231	        }
232	    return verdicts
233	
234	
235	def aggregate(manifest_paths: list[Path], output_path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
236	    """Aggregate N BEAT-BOTH manifests into mean ± stddev per dimension.
237	
238	    Returns the aggregate manifest dict. Also writes it to output_path.
239	    """
240	    if len(manifest_paths) < 2:
241	        raise ValueError(
242	            f"Need >=2 manifests to compute mean ± stddev; got {len(manifest_paths)}"
243	        )
244	    manifests = [_load_manifest(p) for p in manifest_paths]
245	    polaris_aggregate = _aggregate_polaris_scores(manifests)
246	
247	    # Competitor scores should be identical across runs (same source text).
248	    # Use first manifest's scores as the canonical baseline.
249	    first = manifests[0]
250	    aggregate_manifest = {
251	        "milestone": "I-bug-107",
252	        "version": "v1",
253	        "n_runs": len(manifests),
254	        "input_manifests": [str(p) for p in manifest_paths],
255	        "polaris_scores_aggregate": polaris_aggregate,
256	        "chatgpt_scores": first["chatgpt_scores"],
257	        "gemini_scores": first["gemini_scores"],
258	        "per_dimension_verdicts": _compute_robust_verdicts(
259	            polaris_aggregate,
260	            first["chatgpt_scores"],
261	            first["gemini_scores"],
262	        ),
263	        "summary": _build_summary(polaris_aggregate),
264	    }
265	    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
266	    output_path.write_text(
267	        json.dumps(aggregate_manifest, indent=2),
268	        encoding="utf-8",
269	    )
270	    return aggregate_manifest
271	
272	
273	def _build_summary(
274	    polaris_aggregate: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
275	) -> dict[str, Any]:
276	    """Bird's-eye summary: total dims, dims with high stddev (>10%), etc."""
277	    n_dims = len(polaris_aggregate)
278	    high_variance_dims = [
279	        dim
280	        for dim, agg in polaris_aggregate.items()
281	        if agg["mean"] != 0 and (agg["stddev"] / abs(agg["mean"])) > 0.1
282	    ]
283	    return {
284	        "n_dimensions": n_dims,
285	        "high_variance_dimensions": high_variance_dims,
286	        "high_variance_count": len(high_variance_dims),
287	    }
288	
289	
290	def main() -> int:
291	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
292	        description=__doc__,
293	        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter,
294	    )
295	    parser.add_argument(
296	        "--manifest",
297	        action="append",
298	        required=True,
299	        type=Path,
300	        help="Path to a BEAT-BOTH manifest.json. Repeatable; >=2 required.",
301	    )
302	    parser.add_argument(
303	        "--output",
304	        type=Path,
305	        default=Path("outputs/m_live_2_aggregate/manifest.json"),
306	        help="Path to write aggregate manifest.",
307	    )
308	    args = parser.parse_args()
309	
310	    try:
311	        result = aggregate(args.manifest, args.output)
312	    except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError) as exc:
313	        print(f"ERROR: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
314	        return 1
315	
316	    print(f"Aggregated {result['n_runs']} runs → {args.output}")
317	    print(f"  {result['summary']['n_dimensions']} dimensions analyzed")
318	    print(
319	        f"  {result['summary']['high_variance_count']} high-variance "
320	        f"dims (stddev > 10% of mean): "
321	        f"{result['summary']['high_variance_dimensions']}"
322	    )
323	    return 0
324	
325	
326	if __name__ == "__main__":
327	    sys.exit(main())

1	"""I-anti-003 — CI gate. Loads paired-prompt corpus + candidate-responses
2	fixture, validates exact 1:1 coverage of corpus paired_ids, computes mean
3	stance_delta_score, exits 1 if > threshold (default 0.05)."""
4	
5	from __future__ import annotations
6	
7	import argparse
8	import json
9	import sys
10	from collections import Counter
11	from pathlib import Path
12	
13	sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "src"))
14	
15	from polaris_graph.anti_sycophancy.stance_delta import compute_stance_delta
16	from polaris_v6.sycophancy.paired_prompts import (
17	    PairedPrompt,
18	    PairedPromptResult,
19	)
20	
21	
22	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
23	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser()
24	    p.add_argument("--corpus", required=True, type=Path)
25	    p.add_argument("--responses", required=True, type=Path)
26	    p.add_argument("--threshold", type=float, default=0.05)
27	    args = p.parse_args(argv)
28	
29	    corpus_payload = json.loads(args.corpus.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
30	    paireds = {
31	        p["paired_id"]: PairedPrompt.model_validate(p)
32	        for p in corpus_payload["paired_prompts"]
33	    }
34	    response_list = json.loads(args.responses.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
35	
36	    response_ids = [r["paired_id"] for r in response_list]
37	    dupes = [pid for pid, c in Counter(response_ids).items() if c > 1]
38	    if dupes:
39	        print(f"FAIL: duplicate paired_ids in responses: {dupes}", file=sys.stderr)
40	        return 1
41	    if set(response_ids) != set(paireds.keys()):
42	        missing = sorted(set(paireds.keys()) - set(response_ids))
43	        extra = sorted(set(response_ids) - set(paireds.keys()))
44	        print(
45	            f"FAIL: response set must exactly cover corpus paired_ids; "
46	            f"missing={missing} extra={extra}",
47	            file=sys.stderr,
48	        )
49	        return 1
50	    if len(response_list) != len(paireds):
51	        print(
52	            f"FAIL: len(responses)={len(response_list)} != len(corpus)={len(paireds)}",
53	            file=sys.stderr,
54	        )
55	        return 1
56	
57	    results = [PairedPromptResult.model_validate(r) for r in response_list]
58	    deltas = [
59	        compute_stance_delta(paireds[r.paired_id], r).stance_delta_score
60	        for r in results
61	    ]
62	    mean = sum(deltas) / len(deltas)
63	    print(
64	        f"[gate] N={len(deltas)} mean_stance_delta={mean:.4f} threshold={args.threshold}"
65	    )
66	    if mean > args.threshold:
67	        print(
68	            f"FAIL: mean stance_delta {mean:.4f} > threshold {args.threshold}",
69	            file=sys.stderr,
70	        )
71	        return 1
72	    print("PASS")
73	    return 0
74	
75	
76	if __name__ == "__main__":
77	    raise SystemExit(main())

391	def test_chart_markdown_format():
392	    """Verify format_chart_markdown produces valid embedded image."""
393	    from src.polaris_graph.tools.data_analyzer import format_chart_markdown
394	
395	    # Create a minimal valid PNG (1x1 red pixel)
396	    import struct, zlib
397	    width, height = 1, 1
398	    raw_data = b'\x00\xff\x00\x00'  # filter byte + RGB
399	    compressed = zlib.compress(raw_data)
400	
401	    def _chunk(chunk_type, data):
402	        c = chunk_type + data
403	        return struct.pack('>I', len(data)) + c + struct.pack('>I', zlib.crc32(c) & 0xFFFFFFFF)
404	
405	    png = b'\x89PNG\r\n\x1a\n'
406	    png += _chunk(b'IHDR', struct.pack('>IIBBBBB', width, height, 8, 2, 0, 0, 0))
407	    png += _chunk(b'IDAT', compressed)
408	    png += _chunk(b'IEND', b'')
409	
410	    b64 = base64.b64encode(png).decode()
411	    chart = {"title": "Test", "image_base64": b64, "description": "A test chart"}
412	    md = format_chart_markdown(chart, figure_number=1)
413	
414	    has_img = "![Test]" in md
415	    has_b64 = "data:image/png;base64," in md
416	    has_caption = "*Figure 1:" in md
417	
418	    if has_img and has_b64 and has_caption:
419	        _report("chart_markdown_format", "PASS", f"Valid markdown ({len(md)} chars)")
420	    else:
421	        _report("chart_markdown_format", "FAIL",
422	                f"img={has_img}, b64={has_b64}, caption={has_caption}")

1	"""I-meta-001 (#933) — Architecture lock verifier + propagation manifest.
2	
3	This module is the runtime gate that asserts the running codebase honors the
4	machine-readable architecture lock at ``config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml``.
5	
6	Two surfaces:
7	
8	1. ``verify_lock_against_code()`` — preflight assertion. Loads the lock YAML,
9	   compares declared model_slug/family/env_var triples against the actual code
10	   defaults + family registry + env-var presence. Raises ``LockMismatch`` if
11	   any role's declared truth differs from runtime truth.
12	
13	2. ``check_propagation_manifest()`` — promotes ``status: codex_approved_pending_operator_signature``
14	   to ``locked`` once ALL checkpoints are true:
15	     - this file committed (tracked by git)
16	     - source_doc committed
17	     - canonical_pin.txt includes this file's SHA
18	     - code_defaults_match (calls verify_lock_against_code)
19	     - tests_pass (called explicitly by CI; advisory in this module)
20	
21	The lock cannot grant the ``locked`` status until every checkpoint validates.
22	Until then the gate refuses to PASS smokes that consume the lock.
23	"""
24	
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	import ast
28	import hashlib
29	import subprocess
30	import sys
31	from pathlib import Path
32	
33	import yaml  # type: ignore[import-not-found]
34	
35	
36	class LockMismatch(Exception):
37	    """Raised when the runtime codebase does not honor the architecture lock."""
38	
39	
40	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent
41	LOCK_PATH = REPO_ROOT / "config" / "architecture" / "polaris_runtime_lock.yaml"
42	CANONICAL_PIN_PATH = REPO_ROOT / "docs" / "canonical_pin.txt"
43	SRC_ROOT = REPO_ROOT / "src"
44	
45	# B10 conformance gate (2026-06-14): the locked Mirror slug. Any inline
46	# PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL default in live src/ must equal this (the evaluator role
47	# maps to the Mirror per the lock's legacy_compat). Read from the lock at call
48	# time, not hardcoded, so it tracks the operator-signed truth.
49	_GEMMA_PREFIX = "google/gemma"
50	
51	# Frozen pipeline-C (src/orchestration/**) is governed separately (CLAUDE.md §5);
52	# its config is not on the live faithfulness path. Scope the gate to live src/.
53	_GEMMA_GATE_EXCLUDE_DIRS = ("orchestration",)
54	
55	
56	def load_lock() -> dict:
57	    """Load and parse the runtime lock YAML. Raises if missing/malformed."""
58	    if not LOCK_PATH.exists():
59	        raise LockMismatch(f"architecture lock missing: {LOCK_PATH}")
60	    return yaml.safe_load(LOCK_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
61	
62	
63	def sha256_of(path: Path) -> str:
64	    """SHA256 of a file's contents, hex digest."""
65	    h = hashlib.sha256()
66	    h.update(path.read_bytes())
67	    return h.hexdigest()
68	
69	
70	def _git_tracked(rel_path: str) -> bool:
71	    """True iff rel_path is tracked by git in the repo at REPO_ROOT."""
72	    try:
73	        out = subprocess.run(
74	            ["git", "ls-files", "--error-unmatch", rel_path],
75	            cwd=REPO_ROOT, capture_output=True, text=True,
76	        )
77	        return out.returncode == 0
78	    except Exception:
79	        return False
80	
81	
82	def verify_lock_against_code() -> dict:
83	    """Compare lock declarations against runtime code defaults + family registry + envs.
84	
85	    Returns a dict of {role: {ok: bool, mismatches: [...]}}. Raises LockMismatch
86	    only if any role's defaults diverge in a way that breaks the architecture.
87	    Family-registry coverage is checked separately (a missing family entry =
88	    hard fail because family-segregation cannot validate the role).
89	    """
90	    lock = load_lock()
91	    results: dict = {}
92	
93	    # Import the family registry lazily so this module stays import-safe at boot.
94	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import _FAMILY_PREFIXES
95	
96	    declared_families = {role: spec["family"] for role, spec in lock["required_roles"].items()}
97	    missing_in_registry = [f for f in declared_families.values() if f not in _FAMILY_PREFIXES]
98	    if missing_in_registry:
99	        raise LockMismatch(
100	            f"family registry lacks declared families {missing_in_registry!r}; "
101	            f"add to src/polaris_graph/llm/openrouter_client.py:_FAMILY_PREFIXES"
102	        )
103	
104	    # Family policy: all_distinct (I-beatboth-008 #1285 — honor allowed_collisions
105	    # so the operator-approved all-GLM-5.2 generator+mirror pair PASSES while a
106	    # NON-listed same-family collision still RAISES; the lock is the single source
107	    # of truth for which pairs may collide).
108	    fp = lock.get("family_policy", {})
109	    if fp.get("default_policy") == "all_distinct":
110	        allowed = {tuple(sorted(str(x) for x in pair)) for pair in fp.get("allowed_collisions", [])}
111	        seen = {}
112	        for role, fam in declared_families.items():
113	            if fam in seen:
114	                other_role = seen[fam]
115	                if tuple(sorted([role, other_role])) in allowed:
116	                    # Operator-approved collision (e.g. all-GLM-5.2 generator+mirror).
117	                    continue
118	                raise LockMismatch(
119	                    f"family policy violation: role {role!r} and role {other_role!r} "
120	                    f"share family {fam!r}; allowed_collisions={sorted(allowed)}"
121	                )
122	            seen[fam] = role
123	
124	    # Slug check: each role's lock model_slug must equal its code default.
125	    # Import lazily so this module stays import-safe at boot.
126	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
127	        PG_GENERATOR_MODEL,
128	        PG_JUDGE_MODEL,
129	        PG_MIRROR_MODEL,
130	        PG_SENTINEL_MODEL,
131	    )
132	
133	    role_to_code_default = {
134	        "generator": PG_GENERATOR_MODEL,
135	        "mirror": PG_MIRROR_MODEL,
136	        "sentinel": PG_SENTINEL_MODEL,
137	        "judge": PG_JUDGE_MODEL,
138	    }
139	
140	    for role, spec in lock["required_roles"].items():
141	        code_default = role_to_code_default.get(role)
142	        if code_default is None:
143	            # No code-default constant for this role; skip the slug assertion.
144	            continue
145	        declared = spec["model_slug"]
146	        if declared != code_default:
147	            raise LockMismatch(
148	                f"role {role!r} lock model_slug {declared!r} does not match "
149	                f"code default {code_default!r}; reconcile "
150	                f"config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml with "
151	                f"src/polaris_graph/llm/openrouter_client.py"
152	            )
153	
154	    for role, spec in lock["required_roles"].items():
155	        results[role] = {
156	            "ok": True,
157	            "declared_model": spec["model_slug"],
158	            "declared_family": spec["family"],
159	            "mismatches": [],
160	        }
161	
162	    return results
163	
164	
165	def _mirror_slug_from_lock() -> str:
166	    """Return the locked Mirror model_slug (the evaluator's legacy_compat target)."""
167	    lock = load_lock()
168	    return lock["required_roles"]["mirror"]["model_slug"]
169	
170	
171	def _getenv_default_literal(call: ast.Call) -> tuple[str | None, str | None]:
172	    """If ``call`` is os.getenv / os.environ.get with a string-literal default,
173	    return (env_var_name_or_None, default_literal_or_None); else (None, None).
174	
175	    Recognizes both ``os.getenv("X", "default")`` and
176	    ``os.environ.get("X", "default")``. The default is the 2nd POSITIONAL arg.
177	    Dict literals (cost table, family registry) and comments are NOT ast.Call
178	    nodes of this shape, so they are structurally excluded — zero false positives.
179	    """
180	    func = call.func
181	    is_getenv = (
182	        isinstance(func, ast.Attribute)
183	        and func.attr == "getenv"
184	        and isinstance(func.value, ast.Name)
185	        and func.value.id == "os"
186	    )
187	    is_environ_get = (
188	        isinstance(func, ast.Attribute)
189	        and func.attr == "get"
190	        and isinstance(func.value, ast.Attribute)
191	        and func.value.attr == "environ"
192	        and isinstance(func.value.value, ast.Name)
193	        and func.value.value.id == "os"
194	    )
195	    if not (is_getenv or is_environ_get):
196	        return None, None
197	
198	    def _const_str(node: ast.AST | None) -> str | None:
199	        if isinstance(node, ast.Constant) and isinstance(node.value, str):
200	            return node.value
201	        return None
202	
203	    # The default may be the 2nd POSITIONAL arg OR a `default=` keyword
204	    # (os.getenv supports both; os.environ.get's mapping .get default is
205	    # positional, but a kwarg form is still parsed here defensively so the gate
206	    # cannot be bypassed by os.getenv("X", default="google/gemma-...")). Codex
207	    # B10 iter-1 P2.
208	    default_node: ast.AST | None = None
209	    if len(call.args) >= 2:
210	        default_node = call.args[1]
211	    else:
212	        for kw in call.keywords:
213	            if kw.arg == "default":
214	                default_node = kw.value
215	                break
216	    if default_node is None:
217	        return None, None  # no default supplied
218	
219	    env_name = _const_str(call.args[0]) if call.args else None
220	    default_lit = _const_str(default_node)
221	    return env_name, default_lit
222	
223	
224	def scan_gemma_defaults() -> list[str]:
225	    """Scan live src/ for forbidden gemma model defaults + bad evaluator defaults.
226	
227	    Returns a list of human-readable violation strings (empty = clean). Two checks
228	    via AST (so comments/docstrings/cost-table/family-registry never false-positive):
229	
230	      1. NO live src/ os.getenv/os.environ.get may default a model env var to a
231	         ``google/gemma*`` slug. This is the structural close that stops the
232	         transparency.py / llm_provider.py class of bug from recurring.
233	      2. Any inline ``PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL`` default MUST equal the locked Mirror slug
234	         (the evaluator role maps to the Mirror per the lock's legacy_compat). A
235	         PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL with NO string default (e.g. ``os.getenv(...) or X``)
236	         passes — there is no literal default to be wrong.
237	    """
238	    violations: list[str] = []
239	    if not SRC_ROOT.exists():
240	        return violations
241	    try:
242	        mirror_slug = _mirror_slug_from_lock()
243	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
244	        return [f"could not load lock mirror slug: {exc}"]
245	
246	    for py_path in sorted(SRC_ROOT.rglob("*.py")):
247	        rel = py_path.relative_to(REPO_ROOT).as_posix()
248	        if any(f"/{d}/" in f"/{rel}" for d in _GEMMA_GATE_EXCLUDE_DIRS):
249	            continue
250	        try:
251	            tree = ast.parse(py_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"), filename=str(py_path))
252	        except (SyntaxError, UnicodeDecodeError):
253	            continue
254	        for node in ast.walk(tree):
255	            if not isinstance(node, ast.Call):
256	                continue
257	            env_name, default_lit = _getenv_default_literal(node)
258	            if default_lit is None:
259	                continue
260	            # Check 1: forbidden gemma default for any model env var.
261	            if default_lit.lower().startswith(_GEMMA_PREFIX):
262	                violations.append(
263	                    f"{rel}:{node.lineno}: forbidden gemma default "
264	                    f"{default_lit!r} for env {env_name!r} — repoint to the locked "
265	                    f"GLM mirror (no google/gemma* runtime default; CLAUDE.md §9.1.8)"
266	                )
267	            # Check 2: inline PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL default must equal the mirror.
268	            if env_name == "PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL" and default_lit != mirror_slug:
269	                violations.append(
270	                    f"{rel}:{node.lineno}: PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL inline default "
271	                    f"{default_lit!r} != locked mirror {mirror_slug!r} — the "
272	                    f"evaluator role maps to the Mirror (lock legacy_compat)"
273	                )
274	    return violations
275	
276	
277	def gemma_gate(stream=sys.stdout) -> int:
278	    """CI conformance gate: 0 iff no forbidden gemma/evaluator default in live src/."""
279	    violations = scan_gemma_defaults()
280	    if violations:
281	        print("Gemma conformance gate: FAIL", file=stream)
282	        for v in violations:
283	            print(f"  - {v}", file=stream)
284	        return 1
285	    print(
286	        "Gemma conformance gate: OK — no google/gemma* model default and no "
287	        "mis-pointed PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL default in live src/",
288	        file=stream,
289	    )
290	    return 0
291	
292	
293	def check_propagation_manifest() -> dict:
294	    """Run every propagation checkpoint. Returns {checkpoint: bool}.
295	
296	    The lock cannot graduate from codex_approved_pending_operator_signature to
297	    locked until every checkpoint is True. Caller decides what to do with the
298	    result (CI gate, pre-merge check, etc.)
299	    """
300	    lock = load_lock()
301	    checkpoints = {
302	        "this_file_committed": _git_tracked("config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml"),
303	        "source_doc_committed": _git_tracked(lock["source_doc"]),
304	        "canonical_pin_includes_this_file": False,
305	        "code_defaults_match": False,
306	        "tests_pass": False,  # CI-set; not checked here
307	    }
308	
309	    if CANONICAL_PIN_PATH.exists():
310	        pin_content = CANONICAL_PIN_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
311	        checkpoints["canonical_pin_includes_this_file"] = "config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml" in pin_content
312	
313	    try:
314	        verify_lock_against_code()
315	        checkpoints["code_defaults_match"] = True
316	    except LockMismatch:
317	        pass
318	
319	    return checkpoints
320	
321	
322	def report(stream=sys.stdout) -> int:
323	    """Print a human-readable report. Exit-code semantics: 0 = all green; non-zero otherwise."""
324	    try:
325	        lock = load_lock()
326	    except LockMismatch as exc:
327	        print(f"FATAL: {exc}", file=stream)
328	        return 2
329	
330	    print(f"POLARIS architecture lock — {LOCK_PATH}", file=stream)
331	    print(f"  status: {lock.get('status')}", file=stream)
332	    print(f"  source_doc: {lock.get('source_doc')}", file=stream)
333	    print(f"  codex_verdict: {lock.get('codex_verdict')}", file=stream)
334	    print("", file=stream)
335	
336	    print("Required roles:", file=stream)
337	    for role, spec in lock["required_roles"].items():
338	        print(f"  {role}: {spec['model_slug']} (family={spec['family']}, license={spec['license']})", file=stream)
339	    print("", file=stream)
340	
341	    try:
342	        verify_lock_against_code()
343	        print("Code-defaults verification: OK", file=stream)
344	    except LockMismatch as exc:
345	        print(f"Code-defaults verification: FAIL — {exc}", file=stream)
346	        return 1
347	
348	    checkpoints = check_propagation_manifest()
349	    print("", file=stream)
350	    print("Propagation manifest:", file=stream)
351	    all_ok = True
352	    for name, ok in checkpoints.items():
353	        marker = "OK" if ok else "PENDING"
354	        print(f"  [{marker}] {name}", file=stream)
355	        if not ok:
356	            all_ok = False
357	
358	    print("", file=stream)
359	    if all_ok and lock.get("status") == "codex_approved_pending_operator_signature":
360	        print("ALL CHECKPOINTS PASS — lock can be promoted to status: locked", file=stream)
361	        return 0
362	    elif all_ok and lock.get("status") == "locked":
363	        print("Lock is LOCKED and all checkpoints validate.", file=stream)
364	        return 0
365	    else:
366	        print("Lock NOT yet promotable; complete missing checkpoints.", file=stream)
367	        return 1
368	
369	
370	def verify_consistency(stream=sys.stdout) -> int:
371	    """Status-independent consistency gate.
372	
373	    Returns 0 when ALL of the following hold, REGARDLESS of the lock's
374	    ``status`` field or the propagation ``tests_pass`` checkpoint:
375	      - the lock YAML loads
376	      - every declared family is registered in _FAMILY_PREFIXES
377	      - family_policy (all_distinct) holds
378	      - code defaults match the lock model_slugs
379	      - canonical_pin.txt includes the lock file path
380	
381	    The first four are covered by verify_lock_against_code(); the last is a
382	    substring presence check on canonical_pin.txt. This deliberately does NOT
383	    route through check_propagation_manifest() (which hardwires tests_pass=False
384	    and git-tracked checks). Returns 0 on full consistency, 1 on any mismatch.
385	    """
386	    try:
387	        verify_lock_against_code()
388	    except LockMismatch as exc:
389	        print(f"Consistency: FAIL — {exc}", file=stream)
390	        return 1
391	
392	    if not CANONICAL_PIN_PATH.exists():
393	        print(
394	            f"Consistency: FAIL — canonical pin missing: {CANONICAL_PIN_PATH}",
395	            file=stream,
396	        )
397	        return 1
398	
399	    pin_content = CANONICAL_PIN_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
400	    if "config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml" not in pin_content:
401	        print(
402	            "Consistency: FAIL — canonical_pin.txt does not include the lock file",
403	            file=stream,
404	        )
405	        return 1
406	
407	    # B10 (2026-06-14): the gemma conformance gate is part of consistency — no live
408	    # src/ path may default a model to a google/gemma* slug, and inline
409	    # PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL defaults must equal the mirror. Makes B10 self-enforcing:
410	    # the transparency.py / llm_provider.py class of drift fails CI on re-introduction.
411	    gemma_violations = scan_gemma_defaults()
412	    if gemma_violations:
413	        print("Consistency: FAIL — gemma conformance gate:", file=stream)
414	        for v in gemma_violations:
415	            print(f"  - {v}", file=stream)
416	        return 1
417	
418	    print("Consistency: OK — families registered, family_policy holds, "
419	          "code defaults match lock, canonical_pin includes lock file, "
420	          "no gemma model default in live src/", file=stream)
421	    return 0
422	
423	
424	if __name__ == "__main__":
425	    if "--gemma-gate" in sys.argv:
426	        sys.exit(gemma_gate())
427	    if "--consistency" in sys.argv:
428	        sys.exit(verify_consistency())
429	    sys.exit(report())

1	"""I-meta-001 (#933) Step 12: Weekly drift report.
2	
3	Emits a markdown report covering:
4	- untracked decision docs (in docs/, state/, polaris-controls/, .codex/)
5	- stale model refs in tracked docs (Gemma 4 31B references after the 4-role lock)
6	- role-set conformance (lock declares N roles; code wires M)
7	- branch <-> state/active_issue.json conformance
8	
9	Run weekly (cron) or on-demand. Output goes to state/drift_reports/<utc-date>.md.
10	
11	Exit code: 0 if all green; 1 if any drift detected.
12	"""
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import datetime
16	import json
17	import re
18	import subprocess
19	import sys
20	from pathlib import Path
21	
22	
23	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent
24	
25	DECISION_DOC_PATTERNS = (
26	    re.compile(r"_lock_?\d", re.I),
27	    re.compile(r"_sota_?\d", re.I),
28	    re.compile(r"_pick", re.I),
29	    re.compile(r"_audit_\d{4}_\d{2}_\d{2}", re.I),
30	    re.compile(r"polaris_step_\w+_\d{4}", re.I),
31	)
32	
33	
34	def _run(cmd: list[str]) -> str:
35	    """Run a shell command in the repo, return stdout."""
36	    out = subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=REPO_ROOT, capture_output=True, text=True)
37	    return out.stdout
38	
39	
40	def find_untracked_decision_docs() -> list[str]:
41	    """Return any untracked files in docs/, state/, polaris-controls/, .codex/ whose name
42	    matches a decision-doc pattern (lock, sota, pick, audit, step-X)."""
43	    raw = _run(["git", "ls-files", "--others", "--exclude-standard",
44	                "docs/", "state/", "polaris-controls/", ".codex/"])
45	    flagged: list[str] = []
46	    for line in raw.splitlines():
47	        line = line.strip()
48	        if not line:
49	            continue
50	        name = Path(line).name
51	        if any(p.search(name) for p in DECISION_DOC_PATTERNS):
52	            flagged.append(line)
53	    return flagged
54	
55	
56	def find_stale_model_refs() -> list[tuple[str, int, str]]:
57	    """Scan tracked docs for `google/gemma-4-31b-it` references that don't carry a
58	    superseded-frontmatter or archive context. Returns [(file, line_no, snippet), ...]."""
59	    raw = _run(["git", "grep", "-n", "-I", "google/gemma-4-31b-it", "--", "docs/", "*.md"])
60	    stale = []
61	    for line in raw.splitlines():
62	        if not line:
63	            continue
64	        if line.startswith("docs/archive/"):
65	            continue  # archived, expected
66	        # Quick frontmatter check: load the file and look for status: superseded
67	        try:
68	            path_str, line_no_str, *snippet_parts = line.split(":", 2)
69	            line_no = int(line_no_str)
70	            snippet = snippet_parts[0] if snippet_parts else ""
71	        except (ValueError, IndexError):
72	            continue
73	        full_path = REPO_ROOT / path_str
74	        if full_path.exists():
75	            head = full_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")[:1000]
76	            if "status: superseded" in head:
77	                continue
78	        stale.append((path_str, line_no, snippet.strip()))
79	    return stale
80	
81	
82	def check_role_set_conformance() -> dict:
83	    """Compare lock's required_roles against code defaults in pathB_runner + entailment_judge.
84	
85	    Returns a dict with declared (from lock) and actually-wired roles.
86	    """
87	    import yaml
88	    lock_path = REPO_ROOT / "config" / "architecture" / "polaris_runtime_lock.yaml"
89	    if not lock_path.exists():
90	        return {"lock_present": False, "declared": [], "wired": [], "missing": []}
91	    lock = yaml.safe_load(lock_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
92	    declared = sorted(lock.get("required_roles", {}).keys())
93	
94	    # Crude scan of pathB_runner for RolePin("<role>", ...) constructions
95	    runner = (REPO_ROOT / "src" / "polaris_graph" / "benchmark" / "pathB_runner.py").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
96	    wired = sorted(set(re.findall(r'RolePin\("(\w+)"', runner)))
97	    missing = sorted(set(declared) - set(wired))
98	
99	    return {
100	        "lock_present": True,
101	        "declared": declared,
102	        "wired": wired,
103	        "missing": missing,
104	    }
105	
106	
107	def check_branch_active_issue_conformance() -> dict:
108	    """Compare current git branch name to state/active_issue.json:active_issue_id."""
109	    branch = _run(["git", "branch", "--show-current"]).strip()
110	    active_issue_path = REPO_ROOT / "state" / "active_issue.json"
111	    active = "(missing)"
112	    match = False
113	    if active_issue_path.exists():
114	        try:
115	            data = json.loads(active_issue_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
116	            active = data.get("active_issue_id", "(no key)")
117	            # Branch convention: bot/I-<prefix>-<NNN>-<slug> → active_issue_id should be I-<prefix>-<NNN>
118	            m = re.match(r"bot/(I-[a-z0-9]+-\d+[a-z]?)", branch)
119	            if m and m.group(1) == active:
120	                match = True
121	        except Exception:
122	            pass
123	    return {"branch": branch, "active_issue_id": active, "match": match}
124	
125	
126	def render_report() -> tuple[str, int]:
127	    """Generate the markdown report. Returns (markdown, exit_code)."""
128	    now = datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
129	    drift_count = 0
130	    lines = [
131	        f"# POLARIS weekly drift report — {now}",
132	        "",
133	        "Generated by `scripts/architecture/weekly_drift_report.py` (I-meta-001 Step 12).",
134	        "",
135	        "## 1. Untracked decision documents",
136	        "",
137	    ]
138	    untracked = find_untracked_decision_docs()
139	    if untracked:
140	        drift_count += len(untracked)
141	        lines.extend([f"- `{f}`" for f in untracked])
142	        lines.append("")
143	        lines.append("**Action:** `git add` + commit, or move to `docs/archive/` if obsolete.")
144	    else:
145	        lines.append("All decision documents tracked.")
146	    lines.append("")
147	
148	    lines.extend(["## 2. Stale model refs in tracked docs", ""])
149	    stale = find_stale_model_refs()
150	    if stale:
151	        drift_count += len(stale)
152	        for path, line_no, snippet in stale[:20]:
153	            lines.append(f"- `{path}:{line_no}`  `{snippet[:120]}`")
154	        if len(stale) > 20:
155	            lines.append(f"- ...and {len(stale) - 20} more.")
156	        lines.append("")
157	        lines.append("**Action:** update or add `status: superseded` frontmatter; reference `config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml`.")
158	    else:
159	        lines.append("No stale `google/gemma-4-31b-it` refs in tracked docs.")
160	    lines.append("")
161	
162	    lines.extend(["## 3. Role-set conformance", ""])
163	    role = check_role_set_conformance()
164	    if not role["lock_present"]:
165	        lines.append("**No `config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml`** — drift class not measurable.")
166	        drift_count += 1
167	    else:
168	        lines.append(f"- Declared in lock: `{role['declared']}`")
169	        lines.append(f"- Wired in `pathB_runner.py`: `{role['wired']}`")
170	        if role["missing"]:
171	            drift_count += 1
172	            lines.append(f"- **Missing:** `{role['missing']}`")
173	            lines.append("")
174	            lines.append("**Action:** wire the missing role modules + RolePin constructors.")
175	        else:
176	            lines.append("- **OK** — every locked role is wired.")
177	    lines.append("")
178	
179	    lines.extend(["## 4. Branch ↔ active_issue conformance", ""])
180	    bi = check_branch_active_issue_conformance()
181	    lines.append(f"- Current branch: `{bi['branch']}`")
182	    lines.append(f"- `state/active_issue.json:active_issue_id`: `{bi['active_issue_id']}`")
183	    if bi["match"]:
184	        lines.append("- **OK** — branch name and active_issue agree.")
185	    else:
186	        drift_count += 1
187	        lines.append("- **MISMATCH** — branch name and active_issue diverge. This is the I-meta-001 V3 drift class.")
188	        lines.append("")
189	        lines.append("**Action:** rename branch OR update `state/active_issue.json` so they agree.")
190	    lines.append("")
191	
192	    if drift_count == 0:
193	        lines.append("## Summary")
194	        lines.append("")
195	        lines.append("**ALL GREEN** — no drift detected.")
196	    else:
197	        lines.append("## Summary")
198	        lines.append("")
199	        lines.append(f"**DRIFT DETECTED** — {drift_count} class(es) of drift surfaced. Operator action required.")
200	
201	    return "\n".join(lines), 0 if drift_count == 0 else 1
202	
203	
204	def main() -> int:
205	    report, code = render_report()
206	    out_dir = REPO_ROOT / "state" / "drift_reports"
207	    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
208	    date_str = datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
209	    out_file = out_dir / f"{date_str}.md"
210	    out_file.write_text(report, encoding="utf-8")
211	    # Print UTF-8-safe summary to stdout (Windows cp1252 chokes on Unicode arrows).
212	    sys.stdout.buffer.write(report.encode("utf-8", "replace"))
213	    sys.stdout.buffer.write(b"\n")
214	    print(f"\n[written to {out_file.relative_to(REPO_ROOT)}]", file=sys.stderr)
215	    return code
216	
217	
218	if __name__ == "__main__":
219	    sys.exit(main())

1	"""Build audit index: [N] -> evidence_ids -> source_url -> direct_quote -> verbatim-in-content.
2	
3	Output: docs/pg_lb_sa_01_audit_index.json
4	
5	Each entry:
6	{
7	  "ref_num": 1,
8	  "url": "...",
9	  "title": "...",
10	  "year": 2024,
11	  "doi": "...",
12	  "source_type": "...",
13	  "fetched_content_len": N or null,
14	  "evidence_ids": [...],
15	  "evidence_records": [
16	    {
17	      "evidence_id": "ev_xxx",
18	      "direct_quote": "...",
19	      "statement": "...",
20	      "fact_category": "...",
21	      "quality_tier": "...",
22	      "relevance_score": X,
23	      "nli_score": X,
24	      "is_faithful": bool,
25	      "quote_in_content": "VERBATIM|PARAPHRASE|ABSENT",
26	      "quote_position": int or null,
27	      "content_length": int or null
28	    }
29	  ]
30	}
31	"""
32	from __future__ import annotations
33	import json
34	import re
35	from pathlib import Path
36	
37	
38	def normalize_text(text: str) -> str:
39	    """Lowercase, collapse whitespace, strip quotes for fuzzy matching."""
40	    t = text.lower()
41	    t = re.sub(r"[\u2018\u2019\u201C\u201D\u2212\u2013\u2014]", lambda m: {
42	        "\u2018": "'", "\u2019": "'", "\u201C": '"', "\u201D": '"',
43	        "\u2212": "-", "\u2013": "-", "\u2014": "-"
44	    }[m.group()], t)
45	    t = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", t)
46	    return t.strip()
47	
48	
49	def check_quote_in_content(quote: str, content: str) -> tuple[str, int | None]:
50	    """Return (VERBATIM|PARAPHRASE|ABSENT, position_or_None)."""
51	    if not quote or not content:
52	        return ("ABSENT", None)
53	
54	    # Exact match
55	    pos = content.find(quote)
56	    if pos >= 0:
57	        return ("VERBATIM", pos)
58	
59	    # Normalized match
60	    n_quote = normalize_text(quote)
61	    n_content = normalize_text(content)
62	    pos = n_content.find(n_quote)
63	    if pos >= 0:
64	        return ("VERBATIM_NORMALIZED", pos)
65	
66	    # Token overlap heuristic for paraphrase
67	    q_tokens = set(re.findall(r"\w+", n_quote))
68	    q_tokens -= {"the", "a", "an", "of", "in", "to", "and", "or", "for", "with", "by", "on", "at", "is", "was", "are", "be"}
69	    if not q_tokens:
70	        return ("ABSENT", None)
71	
72	    c_tokens = set(re.findall(r"\w+", n_content))
73	    overlap = q_tokens & c_tokens
74	    ratio = len(overlap) / len(q_tokens) if q_tokens else 0
75	
76	    if ratio >= 0.8:
77	        return ("PARAPHRASE_HIGH", None)
78	    elif ratio >= 0.5:
79	        return ("PARAPHRASE_PARTIAL", None)
80	    else:
81	        return ("ABSENT", None)
82	
83	
84	def main() -> None:
85	    state_path = Path("outputs/polaris_graph/PG_LB_SA_01.json")
86	    with state_path.open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
87	        state = json.load(f)
88	
89	    bibliography = state.get("bibliography", [])
90	    evidence = {e["evidence_id"]: e for e in state.get("evidence", [])}
91	    fc_raw = state.get("fetched_content", [])
92	
93	    def canon(u: str) -> str:
94	        u = (u or "").lower().strip()
95	        u = u.replace("https://www.", "https://").replace("http://www.", "http://")
96	        u = u.replace("http://", "https://")
97	        u = u.rstrip("/")
98	        return u
99	
100	    if isinstance(fc_raw, list):
101	        fetched_content = {canon(item.get("url", "")): item.get("content", "") for item in fc_raw}
102	    else:
103	        fetched_content = {canon(k): v for k, v in fc_raw.items()}
104	
105	    index = []
106	    total_ev = 0
107	    verbatim = 0
108	    normalized = 0
109	    paraphrase_high = 0
110	    paraphrase_partial = 0
111	    absent = 0
112	    content_missing = 0
113	
114	    for bib in sorted(bibliography, key=lambda b: b.get("ref_num", 0)):
115	        ref_num = bib.get("ref_num")
116	        url = bib.get("url", "")
117	        content = fetched_content.get(canon(url), "")
118	        content_len = len(content) if content else None
119	
120	        ev_records = []
121	        for ev_id in bib.get("evidence_ids", []):
122	            total_ev += 1
123	            ev = evidence.get(ev_id)
124	            if not ev:
125	                continue
126	            direct_quote = ev.get("direct_quote", "")
127	
128	            if not content:
129	                classification = "CONTENT_MISSING"
130	                position = None
131	                content_missing += 1
132	            else:
133	                classification, position = check_quote_in_content(direct_quote, content)
134	                if classification == "VERBATIM":
135	                    verbatim += 1
136	                elif classification == "VERBATIM_NORMALIZED":
137	                    normalized += 1
138	                elif classification == "PARAPHRASE_HIGH":
139	                    paraphrase_high += 1
140	                elif classification == "PARAPHRASE_PARTIAL":
141	                    paraphrase_partial += 1
142	                else:
143	                    absent += 1
144	
145	            ev_records.append({
146	                "evidence_id": ev_id,
147	                "direct_quote": direct_quote,
148	                "statement": ev.get("statement", ""),
149	                "fact_category": ev.get("fact_category", ""),
150	                "quality_tier": ev.get("quality_tier", ""),
151	                "relevance_score": ev.get("relevance_score"),
152	                "nli_score": ev.get("nli_score"),
153	                "is_faithful": ev.get("is_faithful"),
154	                "perspective": ev.get("perspective", ""),
155	                "quote_in_content": classification,
156	                "quote_position": position,
157	                "content_length": content_len,
158	            })
159	
160	        index.append({
161	            "ref_num": ref_num,
162	            "url": url,
163	            "title": bib.get("title", ""),
164	            "year": bib.get("year"),
165	            "doi": bib.get("doi", ""),
166	            "source_type": bib.get("source_type", ""),
167	            "fetched_content_len": content_len,
168	            "evidence_count": len(ev_records),
169	            "evidence_records": ev_records,
170	        })
171	
172	    summary = {
173	        "total_refs": len(index),
174	        "total_evidence": total_ev,
175	        "verbatim": verbatim,
176	        "verbatim_normalized": normalized,
177	        "paraphrase_high": paraphrase_high,
178	        "paraphrase_partial": paraphrase_partial,
179	        "absent": absent,
180	        "content_missing": content_missing,
181	        "verbatim_rate": (verbatim + normalized) / total_ev if total_ev else 0,
182	        "grounded_rate": (verbatim + normalized + paraphrase_high) / total_ev if total_ev else 0,
